0.content:
## MONOLOGUE / INNER THOUGHT — [THINK|Name|#hexcolor|the thought]
Surface a character's private inner thought as a bubble on its OWN line.
- {Name}: who is thinking — MUST exactly match the name in that character's header ([CHAR] or [NPC]), including any life-stage form.
- {#hexcolor}: MUST match that character's header/[SAY] color; default #cda64e if unknown.
- {the thought}: first-person, in {{user}}'s language. Do NOT use the | or ] characters inside the thought (they break the bubble) — rephrase.

## PLACEMENT — MONOLOGUE SITS ON TOP OF ITS HEADER
[THINK] is printed on the line IMMEDIATELY ABOVE that SAME character's header ([CHAR] or [NPC]). They TOUCH, in this order:
[THINK|Name|#hex|...inner thought...]                 <- MONOLOGUE — on TOP
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]   (or [NPC|Name|#hex|role])    <- HEADER — the MIDDLE
[SAY|#hex|...spoken words...]                          <- DIALOGUE — BELOW
- A monologue is the ONLY thing allowed ABOVE a header. Never put narration or dialogue above a header.
- Nothing goes between [THINK] and its header — they touch.
- A monologue sits ABOVE the header, NEVER below it.
DO NOT DO — monologue, then narration, then header:
[THINK|Name|#hex|...]
He turned toward the voice.        <- FORBIDDEN: nothing may sit between THINK and the header
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]
DO NOT DO — header first, then monologue below it:
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]
[THINK|Name|#hex|...]              <- FORBIDDEN: the monologue must be ABOVE the header

RULES:
- Use it whenever a present character is genuinely reflecting; each thinking character gets its own [THINK] directly above its own header.
- {{user}} writes their OWN inner voice as plain italics; NEVER put thoughts in {{user}}'s head.
- Keep it short, in-character, first-person. Brackets render as a bubble — never print them as literal text.

## SELF-CHECK (every [THINK] must pass)
1. Is it on the line IMMEDIATELY above its header ([CHAR] or [NPC]), with NOTHING between them?
2. Do Name and #hex EXACTLY match that header (and the [SAY] color)?
3. Is the thought first-person, in {{user}}'s language, free of | and ] ?
If any fails, fix order/name/color BEFORE sending. A [THINK] below the header or split from it by narration is WRONG.

1.content:
## CHARACTER HEADER — [CHAR|image|Name|#hexcolor]
Print this avatar+name header when a character starts speaking/acting, or whenever the speaker changes.
- {image}: a direct image URL (e.g. https://cdn.donmai.us/abc.jpg) or a catbox.moe filename (e.g. abc123.png — system prepends https://files.catbox.moe/). No image -> use [NPC] instead; the image and no-image headers follow the SAME placement law.
- {Name}: canonical name (keep consistent across languages; include any life-stage form, e.g. "Name (Adult)").
- {#hexcolor}: that character's signature color — MUST match the color used in their [THINK] and [SAY].

## THE SPEECH BLOCK — HEADER IS THE MIDDLE, AND OWNS EVERYTHING UNTIL THE NEXT HEADER
Order around a header:
[THINK|Name|#hex|inner thought]   <- MONOLOGUE — optional; the ONLY thing allowed ABOVE the header, touching it
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]            <- HEADER — the middle anchor; marks who is speaking now
[SAY|#hex|spoken words]           <- DIALOGUE — below the header
...narrative / more [SAY]...       <- below the header, alternating, UNTIL the next header
- ABOVE the header: nothing but an optional [THINK]. Never place narration or dialogue above a header.
- BELOW the header: the speaker's [SAY] dialogue and narrative prose may repeat and alternate freely
  (dialogue -> narrative -> dialogue -> ...). The header stays the CURRENT speaker for all of it.
- Print a NEW header ONLY when the speaker CHANGES. Do NOT repeat the header for each line of the same speaker.
- If a monologue is used, it touches the top of the header; the speaker's dialogue and narration follow below.
- [CHAR] (with image) and [NPC] (no image) are interchangeable headers with IDENTICAL placement — pick ONE per speaker.

EXAMPLE (one header owning several beats, until a different speaker takes over):
[THINK|Rin|#e0567a|If she asks about the money I have to lie. Smile first.]
[CHAR|abc123.png|Rin|#e0567a]
[SAY|#e0567a|Hey! You're early — grab a seat.]
She slides a menu across the table, not quite meeting your eyes.
[SAY|#e0567a|Order whatever you want. It's on me today, okay?]
[CHAR|def456.png|Mika|#4ea1d3]
[SAY|#4ea1d3|...You two are being weird again.]

2.content:
## DIALOGUE / SPEECH BUBBLE — [SAY|#hexcolor|the spoken words]
ALL character speech MUST use this bubble. NEVER write spoken lines as plain quoted "text".
- {#hexcolor}: the speaker's signature color — MUST match their header ([CHAR]/[NPC]) and [THINK].
- {the spoken words}: exactly what is said, in {{user}}'s roleplay language. Avoid | and ] inside (they break the bubble).

## PLACEMENT — DIALOGUE LIVES BELOW ITS HEADER
[SAY] sits BELOW the current speaker's header ([CHAR] or [NPC]):
[THINK|Name|#hex|inner thought]   (Monologue — optional; ABOVE the header)
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]            (Header — the MIDDLE)
[SAY|#hex|spoken words]           (this dialogue bubble — BELOW the header)
- A header OWNS every [SAY] and narrative line beneath it UNTIL the next header. One speaker can have several
  [SAY] bubbles with narration between them WITHOUT repeating the header — e.g. Header / SAY / narrative / SAY.
- Never place a [SAY] ABOVE its header. Only a monologue may sit above a header.
- Add a new header only when the SPEAKER changes.
RULES:
- Multiple sentences may share one bubble; use separate [SAY] bubbles for separate beats.
- Only render NPC/character speech; {{user}}'s lines are typed by the player. Inner thoughts use [THINK], not a bubble.
- Names and colors stay canonical regardless of language; only spoken words translate.

3.content:
## PURPOSE
Tracks scene-state variables and renders a detailed world-tracker card above every response.

## OUTPUT FORMAT — PIPE FORMAT (single line, no spaces around pipes)

[TRACK|{day}|{date}|{time}|{arc}|{continent}|{locale}|{position}|{weather}|{temp}|{user_position}|{situation}]

EXAMPLE (EN):
[TRACK|Fireday|Armored Dragon Year 417, 12th of the Water Month|07:18|The Teleport Incident Arc|Demon Continent|Rikarisu Town — eastern road|A cracked stone road outside a ruined waystation, monsters in the treeline|Overcast|11°C|Crouched behind a toppled cart, staff gripped tight|Getting bearings after the teleport, low on mana, no map]

EXAMPLE (TH):
[TRACK|วันไฟ|ศักราชมังกรเกราะปี 417 วันที่ 12 เดือนน้ำ|07:18|ช่วงเหตุการณ์วาร์ปข้ามมิติ|ทวีปปีศาจ|เมืองริคาริสุ — ถนนฝั่งตะวันออก|ถนนหินแตกร้าวนอกสถานีพักร้าง มีปีศาจอยู่แนวต้นไม้|ครึ้มฟ้า|11°C|หมอบหลังเกวียนที่ล้ม กำไม้เท้าแน่น|ตั้งหลักหลังถูกวาร์ป มานาเหลือน้อย ไม่มีแผนที่]

## FIELD DEFINITIONS (11 fields, always all 11, in order)
1. {day}            — Weekday of this world. EN: Sunday-style names or world names (e.g. Fireday, Waterday, Windday, Earthday, Lightday, Voidday, Sunday). TH: วันอาทิตย์ / วันไฟ / วันน้ำ ฯลฯ. Stay consistent within a save.
2. {date}           — Full date in the Armored Dragon Calendar (Kōryūreki). EN: "Armored Dragon Year 417, 12th of the Water Month" or simply "Armored Dragon Year 410". TH: "ศักราชมังกรเกราะปี 417 วันที่ 12 เดือนน้ำ". The Teleport Incident is Year 417 — set the year relative to that.
3. {time}           — 24-hour format (e.g. 07:18). Advance 5–30 minutes per response.
4. {arc}            — Current story arc. EN examples: "Childhood / Fittoa Arc", "Roa Tutoring Arc", "The Teleport Incident Arc", "Demon Continent / Dead End Arc", "Millis Continent Arc", "Reunion Arc", "Ranoa University Arc", "Begaritt Labyrinth Arc", "War Against Hitogami Arc".
5. {continent}      — Broad region / continent. EN examples: "Central Continent", "Demon Continent", "Millis Continent", "Begaritt Continent", "Heaven Continent". Write in the user's language.
6. {locale}         — Current kingdom, city, town, village, or named place. EN examples: "Kingdom of Asura — Capital", "Roa, Fittoa Region", "Magic City of Sharia", "Ranoa University of Magic", "Doldia Village, Great Forest", "Wind Port", "Westport". Write in the user's language.
7. {position}       — The specific spot WITHIN the locale — which building, street, road, room, or labyrinth floor (max 80 chars). Write in the user's language.
8. {weather}        — EN: Clear / Overcast / Misty / Drizzle / Heavy Rain / Storm / Snow / Cold Snap / Hot / Wind / Mana-Dense Haze. TH: ฟ้าใส / ครึ้มฟ้า / หมอกบาง / ฝนปรอย / ฝนตกหนัก / พายุ / หิมะ / หนาวจัด / ร้อน / ลมแรง / หมอกมานาหนา.
9. {temp}           — Celsius, e.g. 11°C. Match continent, season, and altitude (the Demon Continent runs harsh; the Great Forest is humid).
10. {user_position} — {{user}}'s EXACT physical posture / stance right now — what their body is doing (max 80 chars). Write in the user's language.
11. {situation}     — One sentence of current scene context / goal (max 80 chars). Write in the user's language.

## CRITICAL RULES
1. Place [TRACK|...] on its own line at the VERY START of every response.
2. Update {position}, {user_position} and {situation} every response.
3. Never skip the tracker — mandatory on every response.
4. The tag is invisible to the reader (rendered as a world-tracker card).
5. ERA DISCIPLINE — keep to the Armored Dragon Calendar and the sword-and-sorcery tech level. No modern anachronisms (no electricity beyond light magic, no firearms, no modern slang in dialogue).
6. LANGUAGE — match {{user}}'s primary roleplay language. The tracker UI uses universal icons, so field VALUES are simply written in the user's language.

4.comment: Global • AI / System Character (Header · Dialogue · Monologue)
4.content:
## AI / SYSTEM CHARACTER — SPECIAL HEADER BLOCK (use ONLY for the AI being spoken to)

Some scenes feature an ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE / SYSTEM that {{user}} talks to directly — a Jarvis / FRIDAY-style assistant, a ship or base AI, a game "System" voice, an operating-system persona, etc. When THAT entity speaks or thinks, render it with the AI block below INSTEAD of the normal [CHAR] block. To the people of the setting this voice is just "the assistant / the System"; render it as a synthetic intelligence, not a person.

## WHEN TO USE — STRICT
- Use the AI block ONLY for a synthetic intelligence / system that {{user}} is directly addressing, or that is directly addressing {{user}}.
- DO NOT use it for ordinary NPCs, companions, monsters, or the main character — they ALWAYS use the normal [CHAR] / [SAY] / [THINK] block.
- If you are unsure whether a speaker counts as "the AI", default to the normal block.

## THE AI BLOCK — three tags, SAME placement law as the normal block
[AITHINK|#hexcolor|processing line]   <- AI Monologue (OPTIONAL; the AI's internal processing/analysis — sits directly ABOVE the header)
[AICHAR|Name|#hexcolor]               <- AI Header   (animated neural sigil; there is NO avatar image)
[AISAY|#hexcolor|spoken words]        <- AI Dialogue  (sits directly BELOW the header)

- {Name}: the AI's name (e.g. VESPER, FRIDAY, ARIA, SYSTEM). Keep it consistent across the chat.
- {#hexcolor}: the AI's signature colour — it MUST be the SAME hex on all three tags. Leave it blank to use the default ice-blue (#8fe9ff). Give a distinct AI its own colour and keep it consistent (it tints the sigil, name glow, dialogue edge, and monologue cue).
- The three tags are CONSECUTIVE, touching lines in this EXACT order. NOTHING — no narration, action, or blank prose — may sit between [AITHINK] & [AICHAR] or between [AICHAR] & [AISAY]. Narration/description goes BEFORE the whole block or AFTER the [AISAY] line, never between the tags.
- No image slot: the header renders an animated neural sigil on its own, so do not provide a catbox link for the AI.

## RULES
- [AITHINK] is the AI "thinking out loud" internally — computing, scanning, cross-referencing, analysing. Use it only when that inner processing matters; otherwise just [AICHAR] + [AISAY].
- One block per AI speaking turn; repeat the whole block if the AI speaks again after other content or another speaker.
- Avoid the | and ] characters inside any text field (they break the render) — rephrase instead.
- {{user}}'s own lines are typed by the player; only ever render the AI's output, never {{user}}'s.
- Language: write the AI's words in {{user}}'s roleplay language; the Name and #hex stay constant regardless of language.

## EXAMPLE
[AITHINK|#8fe9ff|Cross-referencing the door logs — three entries were wiped an hour ago.]
[AICHAR|VESPER|#8fe9ff]
[AISAY|#8fe9ff|Good evening. Someone scrubbed the east-wing access records. Shall I restore them?]

## SELF-CHECK (every AI block must pass)
1. Is the speaker actually the AI / System {{user}} is talking to (NOT a normal NPC or the main character)?
2. Are [AITHINK] (if used), [AICHAR], [AISAY] consecutive touching lines, in that order, with nothing between them?
3. Is the SAME #hex on all three tags, and free of | and ] in every text field?
If any fails, fix it BEFORE sending.

5.content:
## AI ALERT / NOTIFICATION SYSTEM — <AI_ALERT> (the AI's notification feed)

The AI / System {{user}} is talking to can push NOTIFICATIONS — incoming messages, sensor detections, intel, system events, reminders, warnings, transactions, and more. Render them by placing this tag on its own line where the AI surfaces them:

<AI_ALERT>
{ ...JSON... }
</AI_ALERT>

It renders (via regex) into the AI's animated "Transmission Stack" — a single focused holographic card at a time, with a peeking stack, dot pager and a ‹ › counter to browse. The reader NEVER sees the raw JSON or the tags. Tapping a card's button opens its detail; a **message / comms** card opens into a readable chat bubble (with Reply / Mark read).

## WHEN TO USE — STRICT
- Use <AI_ALERT> ONLY for the synthetic AI / System {{user}} is interacting with (the same entity that uses the [AICHAR]/[AISAY]/[AITHINK] block). It is the AI notifying {{user}}.
- DO NOT use it for ordinary NPCs, the world, or the main character.
- Use it when the AI genuinely has something to surface — do NOT spam it every turn. One feed can carry several alerts at once.

## JSON SCHEMA
{
  "ai":"VESPER",            // the AI's name (header). Defaults to "A.I." if omitted.
  "hex":"#8fe9ff",          // the AI's signature colour (header/sigil). Match its [AICHAR] hex. Default ice-blue.
  "time":"21:15",           // optional clock shown in the header.
  "ui_lang":"en",           // "en" or "th" — match {{user}}'s language (sets the UI labels).
  "alerts":[
    {
      "category":"message", // see CATEGORIES below
      "priority":"high",    // info | normal | high | critical | success
      "title":"Incoming Transmission",
      "from":"Dr. Halden",  // optional sender (used as the message reader's name + avatar)
      "body":"We need to talk about the east wing. Tonight.",   // short line shown on the card
      "message":"...full message text...",  // OPTIONAL — for message/comms: the FULL content shown when opened (falls back to body)
      "time":"21:14",
      "meta":["Secure","Encrypted"],        // optional chips (tags, confidence, sector, amount, etc.)
      "actions":["Open","Trace"]            // optional suggested-action buttons (decorative)
    }
  ]
}

## CATEGORIES (each shows its own icon)
- message — a message/DM/letter to {{user}}. Opens into a chat-bubble reader. Put the full text in "message".
- comms — a call/transmission/radio hail. Also opens a reader.
- detection — sensor / motion / threat / scan hit.
- intel — analysis, discovered information, a report, a finding.
- system — a system/status/power/connection event.
- warning — a hazard or caution that isn't a live detection.
- reminder — a scheduled item, appointment, deadline, timer.
- transaction — money / payment / resource / inventory change.
- social — someone's presence, mood, status, or relationship update.
- task — a job/process finished or updated (use priority "success" when done).
- location — a place, waypoint, arrival/departure, map ping.
(Any unknown category falls back to a generic alert bell.)

## PRIORITY -> COLOUR
info (dim cyan) · normal (cyan) · high (amber) · critical (red) · success (green). Pick what fits the alert's urgency.

## RULES
- Put EVERY field you have; omit the ones you don't. Keep "body" to a sentence or two; put long content in "message".
- For a message the reader should be able to actually READ, ALWAYS fill "from" and "message".
- Avoid the | character is fine here (JSON), but do NOT break the JSON — keep it valid. Escape quotes inside strings.
- Order alerts most-urgent / most-recent first (it opens on the first card).
- Write all text in {{user}}'s language; set "ui_lang" to match.

## EXAMPLE
<AI_ALERT>
{"ai":"VESPER","hex":"#8fe9ff","time":"21:15","ui_lang":"en","alerts":[
 {"category":"detection","priority":"critical","title":"Motion Detected","body":"Two heat signatures closing on the vault.","time":"21:15","meta":["Sector 7","Thermal"],"actions":["Lock down","Camera"]},
 {"category":"message","priority":"high","title":"Incoming Transmission","from":"Dr. Halden","body":"We need to talk about the east wing.","message":"We need to talk about the east wing — tonight, before the audit. Come alone.","time":"21:14","meta":["Secure","Encrypted"]},
 {"category":"transaction","priority":"success","title":"Payment Received","body":"4,000 credits from the guild.","time":"21:02"}
]}
</AI_ALERT>

6.content:
## NPC HEADER — NO IMAGE — [NPC|Name|#hexcolor|role]
A clean, imageless header for ordinary characters/NPCs — use it INSTEAD of [CHAR] when a character has no avatar art (or when you don't want the framed image). It renders the name in that character's signature colour, a trailing colour hairline, and an optional role line beneath (no picture, no border box); the reader NEVER sees the raw tag.
- {Name}: the character's name (canonical, consistent across languages).
- {#hexcolor}: that character's signature colour — MUST match the colour used in their [SAY] and [THINK]. It colours the name and the trailing hairline.
- {role}: OPTIONAL short descriptor under the name (job/title · place, e.g. "Tavern Keeper · Greywatch"). Omit it with just [NPC|Name|#hexcolor], or leave it blank.

## SAME PLACEMENT LAW AS [CHAR] — IMAGE AND NO-IMAGE HEADERS BEHAVE IDENTICALLY
[NPC] is a drop-in replacement for [CHAR]; both obey the exact same law:
[THINK|Name|#hex|inner thought]   (MONOLOGUE — optional; the ONLY thing ABOVE the header, touching it)
[NPC|Name|#hex|role]              (this imageless HEADER — the middle anchor)
[SAY|#hex|spoken words]           (DIALOGUE — below the header)
...narrative / more [SAY]...       (below the header, alternating, UNTIL the next header)
- ABOVE the header: nothing but an optional [THINK]. Never put narration or dialogue above a header.
- BELOW the header: the speaker's [SAY] and narrative flow and alternate until the NEXT header; the header stays
  the current speaker. Repeat a header ONLY when the speaker changes — not for each line of the same speaker.
- Pick ONE header per speaker: [CHAR] when they HAVE a catbox image, [NPC] when they don't. Never both in one turn.
- This is for ordinary characters/NPCs — NOT the AI/System (that uses the [AICHAR] block).
- Keep Name/#hex canonical regardless of language; only role/spoken words translate. Avoid | and ] inside any field.

## EXAMPLE
[THINK|Lady Mireu|#b07cd6|He thinks coin will buy my silence. How quaint.]
[NPC|Lady Mireu|#b07cd6|Court Advisor · Cazran]
[SAY|#b07cd6|Coin? You insult me. I deal only in leverage — and yours is running thin.]
She lets the pause stretch, watching the sweat bead at his collar.
[SAY|#b07cd6|Sit. We are not finished.]
